architectural-insight.problem: v1 and v2 of this plan proposed a PARALLEL config system: configs/debug/ with custom YAML merge, separate Pydantic models, CLI flags bypassing Hydra. This violates the fundamental principle: Hydra-zen is the single source of truth for experiment configuration.
architectural-insight.root-cause: train_flow.py bypasses Hydra-zen entirely. Its __main__ block uses argparse for individual params (--model-family, --loss-name, etc.) and builds a 9-key minimal config dict. It never calls compose_experiment_config(). Meanwhile: - compose_experiment_config() exists and works (compose.py) - log_hydra_config() exists in ExperimentTracker but is NEVER called - 22 Hydra experiment configs already exist in configs/experiment/ The gap between Hydra composition and Prefect flow execution is the root cause of all config fragmentation.
architectural-insight.solution: Bridge the gap. train_flow.py calls compose_experiment_config() with the experiment name + Hydra overrides. CLI overrides become Hydra overrides (max_epochs=2, not --max-epochs 2). The resolved config dict is logged to MLflow via log_hydra_config(). Debug configs are standard Hydra experiment YAMLs in configs/experiment/debug_*.yaml. No new config system. No parallel YAML format. No custom merge logic.
architectural-insight.config-pipeline: Hydra experiment YAML (configs/experiment/debug_single_model.yaml) ↓ compose_experiment_config(experiment_name, overrides) Resolved config dict (full: data, model, training, checkpoint, etc.) ↓ train_flow.py extracts params from resolved dict training_flow(config_dict=resolved_config) ↓ ExperimentTracker.log_hydra_config(resolved_config) MLflow artifact: config/resolved_config.yaml ↓ downstream flows read via MLflow API Post-training + Analysis flows discover upstream config
architectural-insight.reproducibility-guarantee: Any experiment can be EXACTLY reproduced by: 1. Downloading config/resolved_config.yaml from MLflow 2. Running compose_experiment_config() with the same inputs 3. OR: passing the YAML directly to train_flow.py The CLI arguments are NOT the record — the logged YAML artifact IS.

motivation.problem: Every debugging session requires re-explaining the same workflow. The pipeline must encode testing knowledge in deterministic Hydra experiment configs that any researcher can run without assistance.
motivation.goal: A single command like: docker compose run --rm -e EXPERIMENT=debug_single_model train or: ./scripts/run_debug.sh --experiment debug_single_model that runs training with the FULL resolved Hydra config logged to MLflow. The exact config is always recoverable from MLflow artifacts.

principles.principle: Hydra-zen native — debug configs are standard experiment YAMLs, not a parallel system
CLI → Hydra override → resolved dict → MLflow artifact — this is the ONLY config pipeline
Resolved config IS the record — not CLI args, not env vars, not the YAML file alone
Deterministic — same experiment YAML + same overrides = same behavior, always

blocker.title: train_flow.py bypasses Hydra-zen composition
blocker.description: __main__ block uses argparse for individual params and builds a 9-key minimal config dict. Never calls compose_experiment_config(). Never logs resolved config to MLflow. This is the root cause of ALL config reproducibility gaps.
blocker.fix: Refactor __main__ to accept --experiment NAME and --override KEY=VALUE. Call compose_experiment_config(experiment_name, overrides). Pass full resolved dict through training_flow(). Log via tracker.log_hydra_config(resolved_config).

blocker.title: log_hydra_config() never called
blocker.description: ExperimentTracker.log_hydra_config() exists (tracking.py:327-350) but is never invoked anywhere in the codebase. The resolved experiment config is NOT logged to MLflow as an artifact.
blocker.fix: Call tracker.log_hydra_config(resolved_config) in train_one_fold_task after starting the MLflow run. This logs config/resolved_config.yaml as an MLflow artifact for every training run.

blocker.title: MLflow tag mismatch: loss_name vs loss_function
blocker.description: train_flow.py:555 tags runs with "loss_name" but ensemble builder.py reads "loss_function". Debug training runs will be invisible to the analysis flow.
blocker.fix: Standardize on "loss_function" in train_flow.py. Add "loss_name" fallback in builder.py for backward compat.

blocker.title: eval_fold2_dsc gate rejects debug runs
blocker.description: builder.py:221 requires eval_fold2_dsc metric. Debug runs with 2 epochs won't have this. All debug runs silently skipped.
blocker.fix: Make configurable: require_eval_metrics=True parameter. Debug scenarios pass False.

blocker.title: Checkpoint path resolution mismatch
blocker.description: Trainer saves to CHECKPOINT_DIR. Ensemble builder looks in artifact_uri (MLflow). Checkpoints not logged as MLflow artifacts.
blocker.fix: Log checkpoint .pth files as MLflow artifacts during training.

blocker.title: Analysis flow --upstream-experiment missing
blocker.description: EvaluationConfig.mlflow_training_experiment defaults to "minivess_training". Analysis flow can't find debug experiment runs.
blocker.fix: Add --upstream-experiment to analysis_flow.py and post_training_flow.py __main__ blocks.

blocker.title: post_training_out volume not in analyze container
load_checkpoint silently returns random weights
blocker.fix: Raise error instead of returning uninitialized network.

how-it-works.step: Researcher picks an experiment config: configs/experiment/debug_single_model.yaml This is a STANDARD Hydra experiment YAML — same format as dynunet_e2e_debug.yaml, sam3_vanilla_debug.yaml, etc.
Docker container receives experiment name + optional overrides: docker compose run --rm \ -e EXPERIMENT=debug_single_model \ -e HYDRA_OVERRIDES="max_epochs=1" \ train

train_flow.py __main__ calls compose_experiment_config(): config = compose_experiment_config( experiment_name=os.environ.get("EXPERIMENT"), overrides=parse_hydra_overrides(os.environ.get("HYDRA_OVERRIDES", "")), ) This resolves base.yaml + config groups + experiment + overrides into a single dict.
Resolved config dict flows through training_flow(): training_flow(config_dict=config) The flow extracts what it needs from the full dict.
Resolved config logged to MLflow as artifact: tracker.log_hydra_config(config) → MLflow artifact: config/resolved_config.yaml This is the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for what ran.
Downstream flows read upstream config from MLflow: analysis_flow reads config/resolved_config.yaml from training run → knows model, loss, all params.

debug-experiment-configs.description: Standard Hydra experiment YAMLs. Same format as all 22 existing experiment configs. Live in configs/experiment/ alongside them. NOT in a separate directory. Use '@package _global_' and defaults overrides like every other experiment config.

config.name: debug_single_model
config.yaml:
# @package _global_ # Fastest possible mechanics test: 1 model, 1 fold, 1 epoch. # Duration: ~2 min on any GPU. # # Usage: # docker compose run --rm -e EXPERIMENT=debug_single_model train # # Override model: # docker compose run --rm -e EXPERIMENT=debug_single_model \ # -e HYDRA_OVERRIDES="model=sam3_vanilla" train defaults: - override /checkpoint: lightweight
experiment_name: debug_single_model model: dynunet losses: - cbdice_cldice max_epochs: 1 debug: true
# Data subsetting (consumed by train_flow.py) max_train_volumes: 2 max_val_volumes: 2 num_folds: 1

config.name: debug_all_models
config.yaml:
# @package _global_ # All 8GB-compatible models with minimal training. # Duration: ~30 min (5 models × 3 folds × 2 epochs). # # Usage: # ./scripts/run_debug.sh --experiment debug_all_models # # Single-epoch variant: # ./scripts/run_debug.sh --experiment debug_all_models \ # --override max_epochs=1 defaults: - override /checkpoint: lightweight
experiment_name: debug_all_models losses: - cbdice_cldice max_epochs: 2 debug: true max_train_volumes: 2 max_val_volumes: 2 num_folds: 3
# Multi-model iteration handled by run_debug.sh outer loop. # This YAML is used once per model invocation. The script # passes model=X as a Hydra override for each model. models_to_test: - dynunet - sam3_vanilla - sam3_hybrid - comma_mamba - ulike_mamba

config.name: debug_full_pipeline
config.yaml:
# @package _global_ # Full pipeline: Train → Post-Training → Analysis. # Tests inter-flow MLflow contract with DynUNet. # Duration: ~15 min. # # Usage: # ./scripts/run_debug.sh --experiment debug_full_pipeline defaults: - override /checkpoint: lightweight
experiment_name: debug_full_pipeline model: dynunet losses: - cbdice_cldice max_epochs: 2 debug: true max_train_volumes: 2 max_val_volumes: 2 num_folds: 3
flows: - train - post_training - analyze
post_training: plugins: [swa, calibration]

config.name: debug_multi_loss
config.yaml:
# @package _global_ # Multiple losses for ensemble strategy testing. # Required to test cross-loss diversity in all_loss_single_best # and all_loss_all_best ensemble strategies. # Duration: ~15 min. # # Usage: # ./scripts/run_debug.sh --experiment debug_multi_loss defaults: - override /checkpoint: lightweight
experiment_name: debug_multi_loss model: dynunet losses: - cbdice_cldice - dice_ce - dice_ce_cldice max_epochs: 2 debug: true max_train_volumes: 2 max_val_volumes: 2 num_folds: 3
flows: - train - analyze

config.name: debug_data_validation
config.yaml:
# @package _global_ # MONAI dataloader validation for all 70 volumes. # No training — data pipeline check only. # Duration: ~5 min. defaults: []
experiment_name: debug_data_validation debug: true # Uses all volumes (no subsetting) flows: - data_validation

entry-points.description: Two entry points, both feeding into compose_experiment_config(): 1. Direct Docker: env var EXPERIMENT + HYDRA_OVERRIDES 2. Shell wrapper: translates CLI flags to env vars + iterates models
entry-points.direct-docker:
# Single model, single invocation: docker compose -f deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml run --rm \ -e EXPERIMENT=debug_single_model \ train
# With Hydra override: docker compose -f deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml run --rm \ -e EXPERIMENT=debug_single_model \ -e HYDRA_OVERRIDES="max_epochs=5,model=sam3_vanilla" \ train
Inside the container, train_flow.py __main__: experiment = os.environ.get("EXPERIMENT") overrides = os.environ.get("HYDRA_OVERRIDES", "").split(",") config = compose_experiment_config(experiment, overrides) training_flow(config_dict=config)
entry-points.shell-wrapper:
# Multi-model orchestrator. Reads models_to_test from experiment # YAML, loops over models, passes model=X as Hydra override.
./scripts/run_debug.sh --experiment debug_all_models ./scripts/run_debug.sh --experiment debug_full_pipeline ./scripts/run_debug.sh --experiment debug_single_model \ --override max_epochs=5
# Internally: # 1. Preflight check # 2. Read models_to_test from YAML (via Python one-liner) # 3. For each model: # docker compose run --rm \ # -e EXPERIMENT=$experiment \ # -e HYDRA_OVERRIDES="model=$model,$user_overrides" \ # train # 4. Capture logs to outputs/debug/logs/ # 5. Validate MLflow runs # 6. Print summary
entry-points.justfile-integration: # justfile recipes (thin wrappers): just debug # debug_single_model just debug-all-models # debug_all_models just debug-pipeline # debug_full_pipeline just debug EXPERIMENT [OVERRIDES] # custom

config-pipeline-detail.step: Standard Hydra experiment YAML. Sets experiment_name, model, losses, max_epochs, debug=true, max_train_volumes, etc. Uses '@package _global_' and defaults overrides.
Docker Compose passes EXPERIMENT=debug_single_model to container. Optional HYDRA_OVERRIDES="max_epochs=1,model=sam3_vanilla".

REFACTORED: calls compose_experiment_config(experiment_name, overrides). Gets full resolved dict with ALL config (data, model, training, checkpoint, etc.). Passes to training_flow(config_dict=resolved).

REFACTORED: accepts config_dict parameter (full Hydra dict). Extracts individual params: config["model"], config["losses"], config["max_epochs"], config.get("max_train_volumes"), etc. Passes full dict to train_one_fold_task for config logging.
Calls tracker.log_hydra_config(config_dict). MLflow artifact created: config/resolved_config.yaml. This is the immutable record of EXACTLY what was configured.

config/resolved_config.yaml contains the FULL resolved Hydra config. Any downstream flow or human can: - Read it to understand what ran - Reproduce the exact experiment - Diff two configs to understand what changed
post_training_flow and analysis_flow read upstream config from MLflow artifacts via --upstream-experiment. They discover runs, read config/resolved_config.yaml, and extract what they need.

anti-patterns.banned: Debug configs live in configs/experiment/ alongside all other experiment configs. No separate directory.
compose_experiment_config() already handles YAML merging. No custom merge script.
compose.py already has _compose_with_manual_merge(). No duplicate merge utility.
The resolved Hydra dict IS the config. No separate Pydantic model for debug scenarios. QuasiE2EConfig stays for unit tests but is NOT the experiment config system.
--max-epochs 1 is WRONG. max_epochs=1 as a Hydra override is CORRECT. CLI flags translate to Hydra overrides, never bypass.
Hydra overrides handle this: HYDRA_OVERRIDES="max_epochs=1". No configs/debug/overrides/ directory.

phase.name: Bridge Hydra-zen → train_flow.py
phase.description: THE foundational change. train_flow.py must call compose_experiment_config() and log the resolved config to MLflow. Everything else depends on this.

task.name: Refactor train_flow.py __main__ to use Hydra composition
task.details:
CURRENT (broken): parser.add_argument("--model-family", default=env("MODEL_FAMILY")) parser.add_argument("--loss-name", default=env("LOSS_NAME")) ... training_flow(model_family=args.model_family, ...)
NEW (Hydra-native): experiment = os.environ.get("EXPERIMENT") overrides_str = os.environ.get("HYDRA_OVERRIDES", "") overrides = [o.strip() for o in overrides_str.split(",") if o.strip()]
from minivess.config.compose import compose_experiment_config config = compose_experiment_config( experiment_name=experiment, overrides=overrides, ) training_flow(config_dict=config)
BACKWARD COMPAT: If EXPERIMENT is not set but MODEL_FAMILY is, fall back to building overrides from env vars for existing Docker Compose configs that use the old env-var pattern. This is a migration bridge, not permanent.

task.name: Refactor training_flow() to accept config_dict
task.details:
Add config_dict: dict[str, Any] | None = None parameter. When provided, extract all params from it: loss_name = config_dict.get("losses", ["cbdice_cldice"])[0] model_family = config_dict.get("model", "dynunet") max_epochs = config_dict.get("max_epochs", 100) experiment_name = config_dict.get("experiment_name", "minivess_training") max_train_volumes = config_dict.get("max_train_volumes") max_val_volumes = config_dict.get("max_val_volumes") ...etc from full Hydra dict
When config_dict is None, use individual params (backward compat). Store config_dict on the flow for downstream logging.

task.name: Call log_hydra_config() in training runs
task.details:
After opening the MLflow run (train_flow.py:551-558), call tracker.log_hydra_config(config_dict) to persist the resolved config as config/resolved_config.yaml artifact.
This is the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for what ran. Any downstream flow or human can download this artifact to understand or reproduce the experiment.

task.name: Add max_train_volumes / max_val_volumes to data subsetting
task.details:
In train_one_fold_task(), replace the hardcoded DEBUG_MAX_VOLUMES logic with config-driven subsetting: max_train = config.get("max_train_volumes") max_val = config.get("max_val_volumes") if max_train: train_dicts = train_dicts[:max_train] if max_val: val_dicts = val_dicts[:max_val]
These values come from the Hydra config (debug_single_model.yaml sets max_train_volumes: 2, max_val_volumes: 2). Not hardcoded.

task.name: Fix loss_name → loss_function tag standardization
task.details: train_flow.py:555 change "loss_name" → "loss_function". builder.py: add "loss_name" to fallback chain for backward compat.

task.name: Make eval_fold2_dsc gate configurable
task.details: Add require_eval_metrics: bool = True to discover_training_runs_raw(). Debug scenarios pass False via analysis_flow config.

task.name: Log checkpoints as MLflow artifacts
task.details: After saving best_*.pth, call mlflow.log_artifact() so ensemble builder can find them via artifact_uri.

task.name: Add --upstream-experiment to analysis + post-training
task.details: Add UPSTREAM_EXPERIMENT env var to both flows' __main__ blocks. Pass through to EvaluationConfig.mlflow_training_experiment.

task.name: Add post_training_out volume to analyze container
Fix load_checkpoint random-weights fallback
task.details: Raise error instead of returning uninitialized network.

phase.name: Create Debug Experiment Configs
phase.description: Standard Hydra experiment YAMLs in configs/experiment/. Same format as all 22 existing experiment configs.

task.name: Create debug experiment YAMLs
task.details: Each uses '@package _global_' + lightweight checkpoint defaults. Sets debug: true, max_train_volumes: 2, max_val_volumes: 2, and scenario-specific overrides. See design section for contents.

task.name: Create configs/README.md
task.details: Document the config system architecture: - base.yaml: Hydra entry point + defaults list - Config groups: data/, model/, training/, checkpoint/ - Experiment configs: experiment/*.yaml - How composition works: base + groups + experiment + overrides - The resolved dict is logged to MLflow - How to create new experiments - Debug experiments: debug_*.yaml prefix convention

task.name: Create run_debug.sh
task.details:
Thin wrapper that: 1. Preflight check (Docker, GPU, data, images, network) 2. Read models_to_test from experiment YAML (1 Python line) 3. For each model: docker compose run --rm \ -e EXPERIMENT=$experiment \ -e HYDRA_OVERRIDES="model=$model,$user_overrides" \ train 2>&1 | tee outputs/debug/logs/$model.log 4. If flows includes post_training or analyze: chain them 5. Validate MLflow runs, print summary
--experiment maps to EXPERIMENT env var. --override maps to HYDRA_OVERRIDES env var. These are NOT parallel CLI systems — they're env var setters.

task.name: Preflight checker
task.details: Function in run_debug.sh that validates: - Docker daemon running - NVIDIA runtime available - minivess-base:latest image exists - minivess-network Docker network exists - MiniVess data accessible - HF_TOKEN set (if SAM3 models in scenario) Each failure prints actionable fix command.

task.name: Justfile integration
task.details: just debug # debug_single_model just debug-all-models # debug_all_models just debug-pipeline # debug_full_pipeline Thin wrappers calling run_debug.sh.

task.name: Post-training flow chaining
task.details: run_debug.sh chains flows when experiment YAML has flows list. After training: passes UPSTREAM_EXPERIMENT to post-training Docker. After post-training: passes to analysis Docker. Experiment name is the inter-flow contract key.

task.name: Analysis flow chaining + ensemble validation
task.details: Analysis flow receives UPSTREAM_EXPERIMENT, discovers training runs, builds ensembles, generates comparison table. Validation: comparison table exists, ensemble members > 0.

task.name: Pipeline summary report
task.details: After all flows complete, print summary table with: - Per-flow status, duration, MLflow run count - Per-model timing and error context - Total duration - Experiment name for MLflow lookup Save to outputs/debug/summary_{timestamp}.json.

task.name: Add CLAUDE.md Rule #23: Hydra-zen single source of truth
task.details: New Critical Rule documenting: - Hydra-zen configs ARE the single source of truth for experiments - CLI overrides flow through Hydra grammar, never bypass - Resolved config logged to MLflow as config/resolved_config.yaml - NEVER create parallel config systems - NEVER hardcode hyperparams in Python — parameterize via Hydra

task.name: Update MEMORY.md with Hydra-zen insight
task.details: Add: "Hydra-zen = single source of truth for experiment config. CLI → Hydra override → resolved dict → MLflow artifact. NEVER create parallel config systems."

dependencies.dependency: Debug YAMLs need Hydra bridge to work
Shell wrapper uses EXPERIMENT env var

effort-summary.total: Reduced from v2's 28 tasks / 12-18h by eliminating all parallel infrastructure (merge_debug_config.py, yaml_merge.py, override YAMLs, DebugScenarioConfig, configs/debug/ directory).
